assets.name: lecture_10_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 is the clearest and most complete frame in this window. The two important board equations, \(\nabla\!\cdot\!\mathbf B=0\) and \(\mathbf B=\nabla\times\mathbf A\), are both fully visible and minimally occluded, while later candidates crop more of the left side or make the board layout less complete. It is the most useful frame for supporting the lecture’s pivot to writing physics in terms of the vector potential.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: If we start writing our equations in terms of the vector potential,
assets.caption_hint: divergence-free magnetic field and vector potential

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 gives the clearest and most balanced view of the board at the moment Susskind refers to the vector potentials in the Lagrangian. The displayed formula is already fully developed in its visible portion, including the kinetic term and the explicit component form of the magnetic coupling, and it is less affected by motion blur or lecturer repositioning than the later frames.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: on the vector potentials here in the Lagrangian.

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 gives the clearest and most useful view of the active derivation. The right-hand board shows the canonical-momentum definition and its evaluation for the magnetic Lagrangian with minimal occlusion, while later candidates either obscure more of the writing, emphasize less-relevant side notes, or block the derivation as Susskind moves in front of it.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: In the Lagrangian, is the second term a kinetic energy or a potential energy?
assets.caption_hint: canonical momentum from the magnetic Lagrangian

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 captures the most useful board state in this window. The derivation has reached a more finished form than in the earlier frames: the x-component equation is written with the bracketed derivative combinations of the vector potential, and the simplified magnetic-field form is also visible. It is less occluded than candidate 02 and less cluttered by side-board leftovers than candidate 04.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: motion of a charged particle in an action form, in the form of a Lagrangian equation

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_06.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the best frame because it captures the most finished board state in this subtitle window. The Hamiltonian line has progressed beyond the bare \(H=\) of the earlier candidates, and the previously derived component formulas for the canonical momenta remain visible on the left. It is the most useful frame for documenting the transition from the canonical momenta to the Hamiltonian construction.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The Hamiltonian is equal to the sum over all the coordinates of the ith component of momentum
